name: Creole Shrimp Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a large, heavy pan over medium heat until fat escapes and the bacon is crisp. Drain off excess fat if desired.
2. Blend in flour and whisk until it reaches a peanut brown color.
3. Add celery, onion, green pepper, and green onions and cook, stirring until vegetables soften, about 10 minutes.
4. Blend in tomato paste, seafood stock, water, lemon juice, sugar, and Creole spices to taste.
5. Stir to blend the mixture into a roux. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover, and simmer very slowly, stirring frequently, until the sauce thickens, about 10 minutes.
6. Add uncooked, shelled, deveined shrimp and parsley. Continue cooking over low heat, just until shrimp turn pink.
7. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
8. Serve hot over rice. Makes 4 servings.
